builderInsights.suggestedTitle: The future of AI education is self-paced and personalized
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: Karpathy shares his vision for AI-powered education: instead of one-size-fits-all lectures, future learning will adapt to each student's pace and understanding. He argues current LLMs are already capable enough to create compelling personalized curricula.

builderInsights.suggestedTitle: Foundation agents: from language to physical world
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: Fan outlines NVIDIA's approach to building foundation agents that can operate in both digital and physical environments. The key insight: treating robotics, game AI, and web agents as a single continuum rather than separate problems.

builderInsights.suggestedTitle: AI engineering is becoming its own discipline
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: Swyx argues that 'AI Engineer' is solidifying as a distinct role — neither ML researcher nor traditional software engineer. The skillset centers on prompt engineering, evaluation, and orchestration rather than model training.

podcastHighlights.title: The State of AI Agents in 2025
podcastHighlights.suggestedTitle: AI agents are finally becoming useful — here's what changed
podcastHighlights.suggestedSummary: Deep dive into why 2025 is the year AI agents went from demos to production. The hosts discuss tool-use improvements, better planning capabilities, and the role of evals in making agents reliable enough for enterprise adoption.

blogUpdates.title: Introducing GPT-5 Turbo
blogUpdates.author: OpenAI Team
blogUpdates.suggestedTitle: GPT-5 Turbo: faster, cheaper, and more capable
blogUpdates.suggestedSummary: OpenAI launches GPT-5 Turbo with 2x speed improvement and 40% cost reduction. Key upgrades include native tool use, improved instruction following, and a 256k context window.
